text: Answer FOUR of the ten essay questions.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write your answers using the Inspera Scan Paper provided.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Note: Inspera Scan question codes MUST be noted during the exam.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer FOUR of the ten essays.
content: Discuss the different types of electrical signals that can be measured in dendrites, their origins, and why they are important.
Discuss the significance of the spatial and temporal features during activation of G-protein coupled receptors.

Discuss how nociceptive signals are processed, relayed and modulated through the main nociceptive pathways. Include a critical account of how specific spinal cord lesions have informed our understanding of the main nociceptive pathways.
Discuss relationships between sound frequencies and azimuthal localisation cues, and how those might differ in smaller versus larger mammalian species.
Discuss the function of the primary visual cortex in vision, comparing its neuronal response properties with those of lower-level and higher-level visual areas.
Discuss the dynamic properties of growth cones, and their contribution to the formation of specific patterns of synaptic connections.
Discuss the processes involved in feedforward motor control. To what extent are these processes localised to specific parts of the motor system?

Choose an example of behavioural learning and memory which demonstrates the extent to which LTP could explain memory formation. What additional evidence would be required to conclude that LTP mediates behavioural change?
How does emotional learning act to support motivated behaviour?
Compare the insights that have been gained from lesion studies and neuroimaging in our understanding of how language is processed.
